---
title: "Random Forest - Randomizes Grid Search Run #1"
author: "Kirsten Dimmler"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    self-contained: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(GGally)
library(purrr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

# Random Forest Run 1

## Read in the data and prep dataframe for plotting

```{r}
df <- read_csv('results/iteration1/random_forest/all_models.csv')

df_parallel <- df %>%
  mutate(
    max_features = as.numeric(factor(max_features, levels = c("0.5", "sqrt", "log2"))),
    criterion = as.numeric(factor(criterion, levels = c("gini", "entropy"))),
    max_depth = as.numeric(max_depth),
    n_estimators = as.numeric(n_estimators),
    min_samples_split = as.numeric(min_samples_split),
    min_samples_leaf = as.numeric(min_samples_leaf)
  )
```

## Get scaling map

```{r}
# Assuming df_parallel has your hyperparameters
hyperparams <- df_parallel %>%
  select(n_estimators, max_depth, min_samples_split, min_samples_leaf, max_features, criterion)

make_scale_map <- function(param_vector, param_name) {
  values <- unique(param_vector)
  tibble(
    parameter = param_name,
    original = values,
    scaled = (values - mean(param_vector, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(param_vector, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

scale_maps <- map2_dfr(hyperparams, names(hyperparams), make_scale_map)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Scaling Map for Hyperparameters

```{r}
kable(scale_maps, digits = 3, caption = "Scaling Map") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```
:::

## All Models

```{r}
### Training

mid <- (min(df_parallel$cv_f1) + max(df_parallel$cv_f1)) / 2
max <- max(df_parallel$cv_f1)
min <- min(df_parallel$cv_f1)

ggparcoord(
  df_parallel,
  columns = 1:6,            # select the hyperparameter columns
  groupColumn = 8,          # color by test_f1
  scale = "std",      # normalize values from 0 to 1
  alpha = 0.3
) +
scale_color_gradient2(
  low = "royalblue",
  mid = "white",
  high = "darkorange",
  midpoint = mid,
  limits = c(min, max)  # 👈 force full range of F1 score
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel Coordinates Plot of Hyperparameter Combinations - Training",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "F1 Score"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_TrainingF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Test

mid <- (min(df_parallel$test_f1) + max(df_parallel$test_f1)) / 2
max <- max(df_parallel$test_f1)
min <- min(df_parallel$test_f1)

ggparcoord(
  df_parallel,
  columns = 1:6,            # select the hyperparameter columns
  groupColumn = 9,          # color by test_f1
  scale = "std",      # normalize values from 0 to 1
  alpha = 0.3
) +
scale_color_gradient2(
  low = "royalblue",
  mid = "white",
  high = "darkorange",
  midpoint = mid,
  limits = c(min, max)  # 👈 force full range of F1 score
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel Coordinates Plot of Hyperparameter Combinations - Test",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "F1 Score"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_TestF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Validation

mid = (min(df_parallel$val_f1) + max(df_parallel$val_f1)) / 2
min = min(df$val_f1)
max = max(df$val_f1)

ggparcoord(
  df_parallel,
  columns = 1:6,            # select the hyperparameter columns
  groupColumn = 18,          # color by test_f1
  scale = "std",      # normalize values from 0 to 1
  alpha = 0.3
) +
scale_color_gradient2(
  low = "royalblue",
  mid = "white",
  high = "darkorange",
  midpoint = mid,
  limits = c(min, max)  # 👈 force full range of F1 score
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel Coordinates Plot of Hyperparameter Combinations - Validation",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "F1 Score"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_ValidationF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)
```

## All Models, following filtering for poor balanced scores

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
ggplot(df, aes(x=balanced_score)) +
  geom_histogram()

## 0.5-0.7 are moderately balanced models
## 0.7 and above is strong

df_parallel <- df_parallel %>%
  filter(balanced_score >= quantile(balanced_score, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
```

### Get Scaling Map

```{r}
# Assuming df_parallel has your hyperparameters
hyperparams <- df_parallel %>%
  select(n_estimators, max_depth, min_samples_split, min_samples_leaf, max_features, criterion)

make_scale_map <- function(param_vector, param_name) {
  values <- unique(param_vector)
  tibble(
    parameter = param_name,
    original = values,
    scaled = (values - mean(param_vector, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(param_vector, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

scale_maps <- map2_dfr(hyperparams, names(hyperparams), make_scale_map)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Scaling Map for Hyperparameters

```{r}
kable(scale_maps, digits = 3, caption = "Scaling Map") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```
:::

```{r}
### Training

mid <- (min(df_parallel$cv_f1) + max(df_parallel$cv_f1)) / 2
max <- max(df_parallel$cv_f1)
min <- min(df_parallel$cv_f1)

ggparcoord(
  df_parallel,
  columns = 1:6,            # select the hyperparameter columns
  groupColumn = 8,          # color by test_f1
  scale = "std",      # normalize values from 0 to 1
  alpha = 0.3
) +
scale_color_gradient2(
  low = "royalblue",
  mid = "white",
  high = "darkorange",
  midpoint = mid,
  limits = c(min, max)  # 👈 force full range of F1 score
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel Coordinates Plot of Hyperparameter Combinations - Training",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "F1 Score"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_BSfilter_TrainingF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Test

mid <- (min(df_parallel$test_f1) + max(df_parallel$test_f1)) / 2
max <- max(df_parallel$test_f1)
min <- min(df_parallel$test_f1)

ggparcoord(
  df_parallel,
  columns = 1:6,            # select the hyperparameter columns
  groupColumn = 9,          # color by test_f1
  scale = "std",      # normalize values from 0 to 1
  alpha = 0.3
) +
scale_color_gradient2(
  low = "royalblue",
  mid = "white",
  high = "darkorange",
  midpoint = mid,
  limits = c(min, max)  # 👈 force full range of F1 score
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel Coordinates Plot of Hyperparameter Combinations - Test",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "F1 Score"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_BSfilter_TestF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Validation

mid = (min(df_parallel$val_f1) + max(df_parallel$val_f1)) / 2
min = min(df_parallel$val_f1)
max = max(df_parallel$val_f1)

ggparcoord(
  df_parallel,
  columns = 1:6,            # select the hyperparameter columns
  groupColumn = 18,          # color by test_f1
  scale = "std",      # normalize values from 0 to 1
  alpha = 0.3
) +
scale_color_gradient2(
  low = "royalblue",
  mid = "white",
  high = "darkorange",
  midpoint = mid,
  limits = c(min, max)  # 👈 force full range of F1 score
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel Coordinates Plot of Hyperparameter Combinations - Validation",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "F1 Score"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_BSfilter_ValidationF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)
```

## Top 5 Models

```{r}
### Training

obs <- length(df_parallel$n_estimators)

quant <- (100 - (100*(5/obs)))/100

df_plot <- df_parallel %>%
  mutate(model_group = ifelse(cv_f1 >= quantile(cv_f1, quant), "Top", "Other")) %>%
  arrange(model_group)  # ensures "Other" comes first, "Top" comes last

train <- df_plot |> filter(model_group == 'Top')

train$run <- 1

write_csv(train, '../RF_summaries2/RF1_Top5_Training.csv')

ggparcoord(
  df_plot,
  columns = 1:6,
  groupColumn = "model_group",
  scale = "std",
  alpha = 1
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Top" = "red", "Other" = "gray80")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Top Five Models - Training",
       x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "Group")

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_Top5_TrainingF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Test

df_plot <- df_parallel %>%
  mutate(model_group = ifelse(test_f1 >= quantile(test_f1, quant), "Top", "Other")) %>%
  arrange(model_group)  # ensures "Other" comes first, "Top" comes last

test <- df_plot |> filter(model_group == 'Top')

test$run <- 1

write_csv(test, '../RF_summaries2/RF1_Top5_Test.csv')

ggparcoord(
  df_plot,
  columns = 1:6,
  groupColumn = "model_group",
  scale = "std",
  alpha = 1
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Top" = "red", "Other" = "gray80")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Top Five Models - Test",
       x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "Group")

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_Top5_TestF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Validation

df_plot <- df_parallel %>%
  mutate(model_group = ifelse(val_f1 >= quantile(val_f1, quant), "Top", "Other")) %>%
  arrange(model_group)  # ensures "Other" comes first, "Top" comes last

val <- df_plot |> filter(model_group == 'Top')

val$run <- 1

write_csv(val, '../RF_summaries2/RF1_Top5_Validation.csv')

ggparcoord(
  df_plot,
  columns = 1:6,
  groupColumn = "model_group",
  scale = "std",
  alpha = 1
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Top" = "red", "Other" = "gray80")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Top Five Models - Validation",
       x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "Group")

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_Top5_ValidationF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

```

## Top Model

```{r}
### Training

# 1. Identify the best model
best_model <- df_parallel %>%
  filter(cv_f1 == max(cv_f1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(model_group = "Best")

train <- best_model

train$run <- 1

write_csv(train, '../RF_summaries2/RF1_TopModel_Training.csv')

kable(best_model, digits = 3, caption = "Best Training Model") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# 2. Tag others and combine
df_plot <- df_parallel %>%
  filter(cv_f1 != max(cv_f1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(model_group = "Other") %>%
  bind_rows(best_model) %>%
  mutate(model_group = factor(model_group, levels = c("Other", "Best"))) %>%  # key line!
  arrange(model_group)  # Best is last, drawn on top

ggparcoord(
  df_plot,
  columns = 1:6,
  groupColumn = "model_group",
  scale = "std",
  alpha = 1
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Best" = "red", "Other" = "gray80")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Model - Training",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "Model"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_Top1_TrainingF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Test

# 1. Identify the best model
best_model <- df_parallel %>%
  filter(test_f1 == max(test_f1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(model_group = "Best")

test <- best_model

test$run <- 1

write_csv(test, '../RF_summaries2/RF1_TopModel_Test.csv')

kable(best_model, digits = 3, caption = "Best Test Model") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# 2. Tag others and combine
df_plot <- df_parallel %>%
  filter(test_f1 != max(test_f1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(model_group = "Other") %>%
  bind_rows(best_model) %>%
  mutate(model_group = factor(model_group, levels = c("Other", "Best"))) %>%  # key line!
  arrange(model_group)  # Best is last, drawn on top

ggparcoord(
  df_plot,
  columns = 1:6,
  groupColumn = "model_group",
  scale = "std",
  alpha = 1
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Best" = "red", "Other" = "gray80")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Model - Validation",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "Model"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_Top1_TestF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)

### Validation

# 1. Identify the best model
best_model <- df_parallel %>%
  filter(val_f1 == max(val_f1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(model_group = "Best")

val <- best_model

val$run <- 1

write_csv(val, '../RF_summaries2/RF1_TopModel_Validation.csv')

kable(best_model, digits = 3, caption = "Best Validation Model") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# 2. Tag others and combine
df_plot <- df_parallel %>%
  filter(val_f1 != max(val_f1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(model_group = "Other") %>%
  bind_rows(best_model) %>%
  mutate(model_group = factor(model_group, levels = c("Other", "Best"))) %>%  # key line!
  arrange(model_group)  # Best is last, drawn on top

ggparcoord(
  df_plot,
  columns = 1:6,
  groupColumn = "model_group",
  scale = "std",
  alpha = 1
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Best" = "red", "Other" = "gray80")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Model - Validation",
    x = "Hyperparameters", y = "Scaled Value", color = "Model"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "plots/RF1_Top1_ValidationF1_PC.png",       # File name (can be .png, .pdf, .jpeg, etc.)
  plot = last_plot(),          # Or specify a plot object, e.g., plot = p
  device = NULL,               # Auto-detects from file extension, or specify like "png", "pdf"
  path = NULL,                 # Optional: directory to save file in
  scale = 1,                   # Scaling factor
  width = 6,                   # Width in units
  height = 4,                  # Height in units
  units = "in",                # Units: "in", "cm", "mm"
  dpi = 300,                   # Dots per inch (resolution)
  limitsize = TRUE             # If TRUE, limits size to 50x50 inches
)
```
